metadata.title: Multi-Framework ESG Mappings
metadata.description: Cross-reference mappings between ESRS, TCFD, GRI, SASB, and CDP standards
metadata.purpose: Enable companies to leverage existing TCFD/GRI/SASB/CDP data for CSRD compliance

mapping_guidelines.direct: 100% alignment - data can be used as-is

integration_strategy.step_1: Identify existing disclosures (TCFD, GRI, SASB, CDP)
integration_strategy.step_2: Map to ESRS using this cross-reference
integration_strategy.step_3: Identify gaps where ESRS requires additional data
integration_strategy.step_5: Transform data to ESRS format and validate

esrs_vs_tcfd.scope: ESRS covers all sustainability (E, S, G); TCFD climate-focused
esrs_vs_tcfd.mandatory: ESRS legally binding in EU; TCFD voluntary (but increasingly expected)
esrs_vs_tcfd.materiality: ESRS double materiality; TCFD financial materiality
esrs_vs_tcfd.reporting: ESRS integrated with financial statements; TCFD standalone

esrs_vs_gri.focus: ESRS regulatory compliance; GRI voluntary sustainability reporting
esrs_vs_gri.materiality: ESRS double materiality; GRI impact materiality

esrs_vs_sasb.materiality: ESRS company-specific assessment; SASB pre-defined by industry
esrs_vs_sasb.metrics: ESRS 1,082 data points; SASB varies by sector (avg 26 metrics)

esrs_vs_cdp.scope: ESRS covers all sustainability; CDP focuses on climate, water, and forests
esrs_vs_cdp.mandatory: ESRS legally binding in EU; CDP voluntary investor/customer-driven
esrs_vs_cdp.materiality: ESRS double materiality; CDP investor materiality
esrs_vs_cdp.format: ESRS XBRL digital taxonomy; CDP online questionnaire platform
